channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: The Wolf Among Us 2 Set for 2027
item.content:encoded:
The Wolf Among Us 2 has a release window.

As announced during the 2026 Summer Games Fest presentation, The Wolf Among Us 2 is finally coming, and it will be released in 2027. It has been 13 years since the release of the original Wolf Among Us from Telltale Games and AdHoc Studio.

The game was first announced at The Game Awards in 2019. Development on the title has survived despite multiple layoffs at Telltale and the studio’s majority closure in 2018.

Wolf Among Us Remaster Set for This Holiday Season

After that trailer, it was announced that a remaster of the 2013 game is set for this holiday season. This game features players controlling Bigby, a take on the Big Bad Wolf. You are the sheriff of Fabletown, and you’re tasked with interacting with other creatures from fairy tales as you try to solve a murder. Part 2 will follow up immediately from the events of the original.

Insider Gaming will continue to share more news about the future of The Wolf Among Us 2 whenever more details get shared. Check out everything announced during Summer Games Fest here. Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 Release Window Revealed at Summer Game Fest

A release window for Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 has been revealed at today’s Summer Game Fest. Here are more details.

Stellar Blade Blood Rain Announced At Summer Game Fest

Stellar Blade 2 has been announced at Summer Game Fest, with an extended gameplay segment.

Palworld 1.0 Release Date Revealed by Pocketpair at Summer Game Fest 2026

Palworld 1.0’s release date, improvements, and other details were unveiled today at Summer Game Fest 2026. Here’s what you need to know.

New 007 First Light Mission Revealed At Summer Game Fest

A new story mission for 007 First Light has been revealed at Summer Game Fest, as Bawma has been confirmed to return.



https://insider-gaming.com/the-wolf-among-us-2-set-for-2027/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 Release Window Revealed at Summer Game Fest
item.content:encoded:
Square Enix has finally revealed the next installment in the Final Fantasy Remake Trilogy, with a new Part 3 Revelation trailer arriving at today’s Summer Game Fest event. The title is set to release in Spring 2027 for all platforms.

The final announcement at the Summer Game Fest was a big one indeed, and as promised, it was a single-player narrative title. However, it wasn’t any random game; it was the third installment of one of the most beloved series in gaming, Final Fantasy 7 Remake.

Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3: Revelation Revealed in New Trailer, Simultaneous Release on All Platforms Confirmed

This is the ideal moment for Square Enix to unveil Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3, especially with the release of the previous installment, Rebirth, for the Switch 2 and Xbox Series X/S has already arrived. The trailer highlighted Sephiroth and various creatures that Cloud and his allies will face in the final part of the series. Additionally, we got to see extensive gameplay featuring a range of characters. Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 will be released simultaneously on PlayStation 5, PC, Switch 2, and Xbox Series X/S, allowing players on all platforms to experience the final chapter together in Spring 2027.

The developers rocked up with two more trailers for Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 at the event. Players were informed about traversing High Wind and exploring their most expansive open world yet. Players were also informed about a hybrid battle system that has been refined through all three parts, bringing fluid real-time action while seamlessly switching between party members.

The third trailer provided players with Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3’s release window:

The anticipation for Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 has been intense, with players receiving various hints from the developers. These hints include the possibility of DLC, new gameplay mechanics, a multi-platform release, and much more. However, it took over two years for the developers to finally reveal just the game’s title. Now that the name and trailer have been unveiled, players are eager to see all the new stuff the developers will introduce for the third installment of the trilogy through a deep dive event.

In other news, Final Fantasy 7 Remake director wants someone else to take on the lead role for the Final Fantasy 6 Remake. Additionally, Final Fantasy 7 Remake series director has spoken about the potential for a DLC for part 3, or even a ‘full title’ after that.

What are your thoughts on Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 being revealed at PlayStation’s State of Play event? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Valve reportedly threatening to delist Rainbow Six Siege from Steam. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
